top_signals.title: Samsung-backed AI chip firm Rebellions targets IPO in South Korea next year, CEO tells CNBC
top_signals.summary: Sunghyun Park, CEO of Rebellions, told CNBC that the company is leaning toward a listing on the KOSPI over the KOSDAQ.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Felons, Fraudsters Flog Offensive Cybersecurity Startup
top_signals.summary: A cybersecurity startup dangling millions of dollars to acquire zero-day security vulnerabilities in popular software is run by a pair of far-right conspiracy theorists and convicted felons whose most recent ventures included fake intelligence companies and a now-defunct AI-based lobbying platform they operated under assumed names.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Philip R. Lane: AI and monetary policy
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: Crypto exchange Kraken is trying to become a bank in Europe
top_signals.why_it_matters: Digital asset volatility and institutional crypto positioning changes.

top_signals.title: EU to again vote to extend ‘chat control’ rules
top_signals.summary: So-called “chat control” rules could be revived in the EU, with lawmakers again set to vote on the legislation Thursday.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Digital asset volatility and institutional crypto positioning changes.

top_signals.title: Tesco ‘in talks’ to exit eastern Europe
top_signals.summary: Tesco is reportedly considering cutting loose its business in central and eastern Europe in a move to end its expansion outside of the UK and Ireland. The supermarket giant has hired bankers to explore the possibility of a sale of its operations in Hungary, the Czech Republic and Slovakia, according to the Financial Times. A [...]
